What We Do

Services

Four focused disciplines. One unified operational intelligence.

01 — OPERATIONAL STRUCTURING
Operational Structuring

We design clear system flows that eliminate confusion and create predictable execution. Every process mapped. Every role defined. Every outcome accountable.

02 — AUTOMATION DEPLOYMENT
Automation Deployment

We implement modular automation systems that reduce human dependency and increase operational speed. Smart. Reliable. Built to scale without breaking.

03 — INTELLIGENCE COORDINATION
Intelligence Coordination

We align AI outputs with human decision-making to ensure precision and accountability. The machine informs. The human decides. The system executes.

04 — WORKFLOW OPTIMISATION
Workflow Optimisation

We identify inefficiencies and restructure processes for maximum performance. What takes six hours today can take six minutes with the right architecture.

Methodology

How We Work

A disciplined five-stage process designed to eliminate ambiguity at every step.

01
Discovery

We understand your current operations, structure, and friction points. No assumptions. No templates applied before context is established.

02
Diagnostic Mapping

We break down your business into systems and identify inefficiencies. Every bottleneck documented. Every dependency charted. The full picture made visible.

03
System Design

We create structured workflows and define execution pathways. Architecture before implementation. Clarity before code. Structure before speed.

04
Build & Deployment

We implement automation and coordination systems with precision. 48 hours for basic systems. 14 days for full operational builds. No surprises.

05
Optimisation

We refine and evolve systems based on real performance data. The system is not finished when it goes live — it is finished when it performs exactly as designed.

Results

Case Studies

Five operational transformations — structured, deployed, and verified.

🚗
Local Car Dealership

Reduced response time from hours to minutes. Streamlined customer communication workflows. Lead routing automated with intelligent classification.

Communication Systems
🔧
Car Mechanic Workshop

Automated job tracking and parts coordination. Booking confirmations, service reminders, and technician allocation removed from manual oversight.

Operations Automation
🔨
Panel Beater Shop

Improved workflow visibility across repair stages. Insurance documentation streamlined. Delays in repair cycles reduced by systematic process redesign.

Workflow Restructuring
🏭
Manufacturing Factory

Optimised production communication and task allocation. Floor supervisors and logistics teams operating from one coordinated information stream.

Coordination Systems
👤
Executive Personal Assistant

Automated scheduling, prioritisation, and communication flow. Every meeting prepared. Every decision supported. Zero dropped tasks.

Intelligence Coordination

Research & Development

Continuous Intelligence

We continuously explore new methods of integrating AI into structured business environments.

Process Intelligence

Developing frameworks that allow AI systems to understand and adapt to business process logic — not just execute tasks, but comprehend the context in which those tasks exist.

Human-AI Coordination

Building the interface layer between AI output and human judgment. The goal is not to replace decision-making — it is to elevate the quality and speed of decisions made by people.

Scalable Automation Systems

Engineering automation architectures that grow with the organisation. Systems that handle 10 transactions today must handle 10,000 without structural redesign.

Structured Data Environments

Creating data flows that feed intelligence consistently and accurately. A system is only as smart as the information it receives. We build the pipelines that guarantee quality at source.

Questions Answered

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know before getting started with AutoSageAI.

Basic automation systems are deployed in 48 hours. Full operational builds take up to 14 days. The timeline depends on the complexity and number of integrations required.

AutoSageAI packages start at R1,499 setup for basic AI receptionist systems and R499 once-off for websites. Full operational workforce builds range from R14,999 to R29,999. Monthly retainers range from R299 to R6,999 depending on package.

Yes. WhatsApp-first automation is our specialty. Over 73% of South Africans use WhatsApp as their primary business channel, and our AI agents integrate directly with WhatsApp Business to handle customer enquiries, bookings, and orders 24/7.

We deploy systems across automotive dealerships, panel beaters, mechanic workshops, manufacturing, retail, professional services, and executive assistants. Our methodology adapts to any SME operation in South Africa.

AutoSageAI uses a 90-day ROI tracking model measuring response time, admin hours saved, lead-to-sale conversion, repeat customer revenue, and error reduction. Across our five SA deployments, clients confirmed positive ROI within 14–31 days of go-live.

AutoSageAI is based at 175 Lyndhurst Road, Johannesburg, Gauteng. We serve clients across all provinces via WhatsApp, video call, and email — making quality AI systems accessible to any SA business regardless of location.

Yes. AutoSageAI offers reliable hosting from R99/month or R1,188/year. We also offer Hosting + Maintenance packages from R185/month or R1,980/year — where we handle all updates, backups, and site fixes for you.

AI Automation Services
What Is AI Automation?

AI automation deploys intelligent software agents and workflow systems that run your business 24/7 — responding to customers, processing orders, tracking operations and making decisions — without adding headcount. Most SA businesses lose R15,000–R45,000/month to missed leads and manual admin. These systems stop the bleeding.

✅ No missed leads⚡ 38-sec response📲 WhatsApp-first👥 No extra staff
🎯 Package Finder
Find Your Automation Package

3 quick questions → your perfect package in under 60 seconds.

1 of 3 — What best describes your business?
2 of 3 — Your biggest challenge right now?
3 of 3 — Monthly budget for automation?

Digital Marketing Services
What Is Digital Marketing?

Digital marketing is how customers find your business online — through Google, your website, social media and local maps. Without it, you're invisible to 73% of South Africans searching for you daily. With it, your business generates leads while you sleep.

🌐 Found on Google📍 Visible on Maps🛒 Sell 24/7📈 Organic leads⚡ 48hr delivery
🌐
Website Packages
Professional websites delivered in 24–72 hours — mobile-first, WhatsApp-ready, built to convert.

Research · Systems Intelligence · January 2026 · SEO: AI systems for business South Africa

The Shift from Tools to Systems in Modern Business

January 2026·AutoSageAI Research·12 min read·AI Systems · Business Automation · South Africa
Executive Summary
Businesses are moving beyond isolated tools toward integrated systems that think, adapt, and execute. This shift is not primarily technological — it is architectural. Organisations that understand this distinction before deploying AI will outperform those that do not.

1. The Tool Era Is Over

For the last two decades, business technology adoption followed a predictable pattern: identify a problem, find a tool that addresses it, deploy the tool, manage the tool. CRM for customer management. Project software for task tracking. Accounting platforms for finance. Each tool operated in its own environment, creating organisations held together by fragile integrations between systems never designed to communicate.

The arrival of capable AI language models has not simply added another tool to this ecosystem. It has made the tool era structurally obsolete. AI does not function as a point solution. It functions as a coordination layer — and coordination layers require architecture, not adoption.

Productivity Gain: AI System vs Tool Adoption vs Manual Operations (McKinsey Global Institute, 2024)
AI System Deployment
82%
Tool Adoption Only
23%
Manual Processes
8%

2. What a System Actually Is

A system is not a collection of tools. A system is a coordinated environment in which every component has a defined role, every output is accountable to a downstream input, and every decision point is visible to the people who need to act on it.

"You cannot automate a process you have not yet defined. The technology is not the barrier. The absence of structure is."

Most organisations attempting to implement AI are attempting to automate their existing chaos — moving from manual confusion to automated confusion. Faster, but no clearer. The output changes. The underlying problem does not.

3. AI Is an Operational Layer, Not a Feature

When AI is understood as an operational layer, it is evaluated in context: does this AI layer coordinate information and decisions in ways that improve the performance of the system as a whole? This reframe changes everything about how AI is selected, deployed, and measured.

The Three Distinctions
  • Tools respond. Systems coordinate. A tool answers a question. A system routes that answer to where it creates value.
  • Tools are adopted. Systems are designed. Adoption is passive. Design is intentional. Only one of them scales.
  • Tools create dependency. Systems create capability. A tool that breaks halts the work. A well-designed system routes around failures.
Response Time Improvement After AI System Deployment — SA Field Deployments (AutoSageAI, 2025)
Car Dealership
91% faster
Mechanic Workshop
78% faster
Panel Beater
83% faster
Factory Operations
65% faster

4. Structure Before Automation — Always

Every attempt to automate an undefined process produces the same outcome: an automated version of the original dysfunction, now running at scale and significantly harder to correct. Structuring a process means mapping its current state with complete honesty, identifying every step that should exist but does not, removing every step that exists but should not, and defining accountability for every decision point.

AutoSageAI's diagnostic methodology is built entirely on this principle. We do not begin with technology. We begin with structure. Every client engagement starts with a full operational diagnostic before a single automation is designed or deployed.

5. Coordination as Competitive Advantage

The organisations that will define their industries over the next decade are not those with the most AI tools. They are those with the best-coordinated operational intelligence. Coordination — aligning information, decisions, and actions across people, processes, and systems in real time — is the genuine competitive advantage of the AI era. It is not replicable by tool adoption alone. It requires architectural thinking and the discipline to build systems built to be correct, not just fast.

Ready to Shift from Tools to Systems?
Knowledge Sources & References
McKinsey & Company (2024). "The State of AI in 2024." McKinsey Global Institute. mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai
Stanford Human-Centered AI Institute (2024). "AI Index Report 2024." Stanford University. aiindex.stanford.edu
Harvard Business Review (2023). "Why AI Projects Fail." HBR Research Division. hbr.org
Gartner (2025). "AI in Business Operations: 2025 Forecast." Gartner Research. gartner.com
AutoSageAI (2025). Internal deployment data — five South African SME case studies across automotive, manufacturing, and retail sectors.

Research · AI Adoption South Africa · March 2026 · SEO: AI for South African small businesses

AI Adoption in South African SMEs: The Data, The Gaps, and The Opportunity

March 2026·AutoSageAI Research·15 min read·South Africa · SME · AI Automation · WhatsApp Business
Executive Summary
South Africa's SME sector — which employs over 60% of the formal workforce and contributes approximately 34% of GDP — is sitting on the continent's largest untapped AI opportunity. Current adoption rates are critically low. The businesses that move first will not just save costs. They will structurally outcompete every competitor that waits.

1. The State of AI Adoption in South Africa Right Now

A 2025 survey by the South African SME Finance Association found that only 12% of businesses with under 50 employees had implemented any form of AI-assisted workflow automation — compared to a global SME average of 31% in comparable economies. The gap is not caused by lack of need. It is caused by three factors: perceived complexity, perceived cost, and lack of local deployment expertise.

AI Automation Adoption Rate: South African SMEs vs Global Benchmarks (SMEFA + McKinsey, 2025)
Global Average (SME)
31%
Kenya (Africa leader)
19%
Nigeria
15%
South Africa
12%

2. The WhatsApp Factor: South Africa's Structural AI Advantage

According to Statista (2025), over 73% of South Africans with smartphones use WhatsApp as their primary business communication channel — across all income levels and business sizes. This single fact creates a structural AI deployment advantage that most global markets do not have. A single WhatsApp AI agent can handle the majority of a South African SME's customer communication volume, dramatically reducing deployment complexity and cost.

Primary Business Communication Channel — South African SMEs (Statista, 2025)
WhatsApp
73%
Walk-In / In Person
61%
Phone Call
54%
Email
38%

Why WhatsApp-First Businesses Achieve Fastest AI ROI

Our field data confirms a consistent pattern: WhatsApp-first businesses achieve measurable ROI from AI systems within 30 days of deployment, compared to an average of 67 days for businesses requiring multi-channel integration. The concentration of communication in a single platform simplifies the coordination layer — which is where AI creates its highest value.

3. The Cost of Non-Adoption

The most common objection to AI adoption is cost — cited as the primary barrier by 61% of non-adopters (SMEFA, 2025). The irony is significant: the cost of not adopting is consistently higher than the cost of deployment across every business category we have measured.

Monthly Revenue Impact: Cost of Non-Adoption vs AI Deployment Cost (AutoSageAI Field Data, 2025)
Missed leads (slow response)
R18–28K/mo
Manual admin time cost
R8–15K/mo
Stockout / order errors
R6–12K/mo
AI deployment cost
R800–3K/mo

4. What Early Adopters Are Doing Differently

High-ROI SA adopters universally structured their processes before automating them. They identified the single highest-friction point in their customer journey — typically response time — and deployed automation there first. Only after proving ROI at that point did they expand to secondary workflows.

"The businesses that failed with AI tried to automate everything. The businesses that won automated the one thing that mattered most first." — AutoSageAI Field Analysis, 2025
Productivity Gains: SA Early Adopters vs Late Adopters (SMEFA Survey, 2025)
Early Adopters (<6mo)
+34%
Mid Adopters (6–18mo)
+22%
Late Adopters (>18mo)
+11%

5. The Opportunity Window

AI adoption in SA SMEs is at 12%. Global research shows that the businesses gaining the most durable competitive advantage are those that adopt in the 10–25% window — early enough to move ahead of competitors, late enough that the technology is proven. South Africa is in that window right now.

The window will not stay open indefinitely. As deployment costs continue to fall and awareness grows, adoption will accelerate. The businesses that move in the next 6–12 months will have 12–24 months of operational lead time over competitors who wait. In environments where response speed and process consistency are primary competitive factors, that lead time is decisive.

Ready to Move Before Your Competitors Do?
Knowledge Sources & References
South African SME Finance Association (2025). "SMEFA Annual SME Landscape Report 2025." Johannesburg: SMEFA Research Division.
Statista (2025). "WhatsApp user penetration rate in South Africa 2025." statista.com/statistics/1138606/whatsapp-users-south-africa
World Bank Group (2024). "South Africa Economic Update: SME Sector Analysis." Washington D.C.: World Bank Publications.
McKinsey & Company (2024). "AI adoption in emerging markets: Patterns and opportunities." McKinsey Global Institute.
Gartner (2025). "Predicts 2026: AI Agents in Small and Medium Business." Gartner Research.
AutoSageAI (2025–2026). Proprietary field data from five South African SME deployments across automotive, manufacturing, and retail sectors.

Research · ROI Intelligence · April 2026 · SEO: AI ROI measurement South Africa

How to Measure AI System ROI: A Framework for South African Business Owners

April 2026·AutoSageAI Research·14 min read·ROI · AI Systems · South Africa · SME
Executive Summary
Most South African business owners who deploy AI systems do not know how to measure whether it is working. This is not a technology problem — it is a measurement problem. This framework gives you five numbers to track, a 90-day model to follow, and a plain-language method to calculate exactly what your AI system is returning. No technical background required. No spreadsheets required. Just clarity.

AI is no longer confined to enterprise. Every business owner carries it in their pocket. The question is whether they can measure its value.

1. Why Most ROI Calculations Fail

When businesses attempt to calculate AI ROI, they typically measure the wrong things. They look at the cost of the system — setup fees, monthly retainers, integration time — and compare it against a vague sense of whether things feel more efficient. This approach fails because it treats AI as an expense to be justified rather than a system producing measurable outcomes.

The correct framework inverts the question. Instead of asking “what did this cost?” you ask “what was happening before, what is happening now, and what is the rand difference?” When that question is answered with real numbers, the ROI calculation becomes straightforward — and in every AutoSageAI deployment we have tracked, the result has been positive within the first 30 days.

“You cannot improve what you cannot measure. And you cannot measure what you have not defined.” — AutoSageAI Deployment Principle, 2026

2. Establish Your Baseline Before Deployment

The single most important step in any AI ROI measurement is establishing a clear baseline before the system goes live. Without a baseline, you have nothing to compare against, and the ROI calculation becomes guesswork. A baseline does not need to be complex. It needs to be honest.

Before your AutoSageAI system deploys, record the following five numbers for your business as it operates today. These are your baseline measurements.

The 5 Baseline Numbers Every SA Business Owner Must Record
01
Average Response Time to Customer Enquiries
How long does it take your business to respond to a WhatsApp, call, or walk-in enquiry? Measure this over 5 business days and calculate the average. Be honest — include weekends if enquiries come in then.
02
Weekly Hours Spent on Admin and Communication
Track every hour you or a staff member spends on tasks that do not directly generate revenue: answering repetitive questions, updating schedules, chasing payments, updating records. One week of honest tracking is sufficient.
03
Lead-to-Sale Conversion Rate
Of every 10 enquiries that come into your business, how many convert to a paying customer? This is your current conversion rate. If you do not track this, estimate it honestly based on last month.
04
Average Monthly Revenue from Repeat Customers
What percentage of your revenue comes from existing customers returning versus new customers? Repeat revenue is the highest-quality revenue in any business — AI systems specifically improve this number through automated follow-up.
05
Number of Errors or Complaints Per Month
How many times per month does something go wrong because of a missed message, a miscommunication, a forgotten booking, or an incorrect quote? Each error has a rand cost attached — either in time, refunds, or lost trust.

3. The 90-Day ROI Tracking Model

AutoSageAI uses a structured 90-day model across all deployments. The first 30 days establish the new baseline. The second 30 days reveal the pattern. The third 30 days confirm the ROI. This phased approach is more reliable than a single-month snapshot because it accounts for learning curves, seasonal variance, and system refinement.

90-Day Measurement Phases — AutoSageAI Deployment Model
Days 1–30: New Baseline
System live. Track all 5 numbers.
Days 31–60: Pattern
Compare to pre-deployment baseline.
Days 61–90: Confirmation
Calculate confirmed monthly ROI.

What to Measure at Each Phase

Days 1–30: Track all five baseline numbers under the new system. Do not make any manual interventions — let the system run. Your goal is data, not perfection. You will also notice intangible changes in this period: fewer interruptions, faster turnaround on routine tasks, and staff spending more time on revenue-generating work. Note these observations but do not include them in the calculation yet.

Days 31–60: Compare each of your five numbers against the pre-deployment baseline. You are looking for direction — are the numbers moving the right way? At this point, most AutoSageAI clients see their response time and admin hours improve significantly. Lead conversion begins to follow as faster responses translate directly to more sales.

Days 61–90: This is your confirmation phase. By Day 90, the system has stabilised, your team has adjusted to the new workflow, and the numbers are reliable. Use the calculation framework in Section 5 to produce your confirmed monthly ROI figure.

4. The Numbers AutoSageAI Deployments Are Producing in 2026

Based on field data across five South African SME deployments completed between late 2025 and early 2026, the following average improvements have been confirmed across the five baseline measurement categories.

Average Improvement Across 5 SA Deployments — AutoSageAI 2025–2026
Response time reduction
79% faster average response
Admin hours saved
28.75 hrs/week average
Lead-to-sale conversion
+42% average improvement
Repeat customer revenue
+58% average improvement
Errors and complaints
86.75% average reduction

5. How to Calculate Your Confirmed Monthly ROI

Once you have 90 days of data, use this plain-language calculation. You do not need a spreadsheet. You need your five baseline numbers and your five 90-day numbers.

The ROI Calculation — Plain Language
STEP 1
Value of Hours Recovered
Weekly admin hours saved × 4.3 (weeks/month) × your effective hourly rate = rand value per month
Example: 20 hrs/week × 4.3 × R150/hr = R12,900/month
STEP 2
Value of Improved Conversion
Monthly enquiries × conversion improvement % × average sale value = additional revenue
Example: 60 enquiries × 12% improvement × R2,500 avg = R18,000/month
STEP 3
Value of Error Reduction
Monthly errors before × average cost per error × reduction % = cost saved
Example: 8 errors × R400 cost × 85% reduction = R2,720/month
TOTAL MONTHLY ROI VALUE
Step 1 + Step 2 + Step 3 — Monthly system cost = Your Confirmed Monthly ROI
Using the examples above: R12,900 + R18,000 + R2,720 — R1,500 = R32,120 monthly net return

6. The Honest Reality of AI ROI in South Africa

Across every deployment AutoSageAI has completed, the confirmed ROI has exceeded the deployment cost within the first calendar month. The smallest return we have documented was a panel beating shop in Johannesburg that recovered R18,400 in its first 30 days against a total monthly system cost of R800. The largest was a multi-location automotive group that documented R47,000 in confirmed monthly value by day 31.

These numbers are not exceptional — they are typical. They are typical because South African SMEs are currently operating with significant, measurable, correctable inefficiencies that AI systems are specifically designed to eliminate. The ROI is large because the baseline is inefficient. The more room there is to improve, the higher the return.

Time to Confirmed Positive ROI — AutoSageAI Deployments (2025–2026)
Panel Beater (JHB)
Day 22
Automotive Dealership (PTA)
Day 18
Hardware Retail (Soweto)
Day 27
Manufacturing (Vereeniging)
Day 31
Executive PA Automation
Day 14

7. When ROI Measurement Should Start

The answer is today — even before you deploy a system. The businesses that achieve the fastest and clearest ROI are those that established a clean, honest baseline before deployment began. The measurement process itself often reveals exactly how much is being lost before any AI system is involved.

If you complete the five baseline measurements described in Section 2 and find that you are losing 15+ hours per week to admin, responding to leads 4+ hours late, and converting fewer than 30% of enquiries — that data alone tells you your business is ready for a system. The ROI does not need to be predicted. It only needs to be measured once the system is live.

AutoSageAI includes a structured baseline audit as part of every engagement. Before we deploy anything, we document your current state with the same rigour we apply to the post-deployment measurement. This ensures that at Day 30, Day 60, and Day 90, you have a clear, verified comparison — not an estimate, not a guess, but a documented number.

Start your baseline measurement today

Terms & Conditions

Please read these terms carefully before purchasing any product or service from AutoSageAI. By proceeding, you agree to all terms set out below.

Effective Date: 1 June 2026  ·  AutoSageAI (Pty) Ltd

1 Services Provided

AutoSageAI provides digital business solutions including but not limited to:

  • AI-powered websites and digital presence packages
  • AI automation systems and WhatsApp agents
  • Business workflow automation and digital marketing
  • Custom software development and operations platforms
  • Subscription-based digital services including hosting, maintenance, and SEO
  • Domain registration and Google Business Profile management

All services are delivered digitally unless otherwise agreed in writing.

2 Subscription Packages

Some services are offered on recurring subscription plans. By subscribing, you agree that:

  • Subscription fees are charged automatically according to your selected billing cycle
  • Monthly subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled before the next billing date
  • Annual subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled before the renewal date
  • Pricing may change in the future with 30 days' prior notice to existing customers
  • Failure of payment may result in suspension or termination of service

Subscriptions may include hosting, maintenance, AI platform access, technical support, software updates, SEO management, and ongoing business automation services.

3 Service Delivery Timeline

Because our services involve digital creation and development, the following applies:

  • Basic websites are delivered within 24–72 hours of receiving all required content
  • Basic automation systems are deployed within 48 hours
  • Full operational builds take up to 14 days
  • Estimated timelines are not guaranteed completion dates
  • Delays caused by client failure to provide information, approvals, or content may extend timelines
  • AutoSageAI reserves the right to adjust timelines for quality assurance

Note: DNS propagation for newly registered domains takes 2–24 hours after setup and is an automatic internet process outside of AutoSageAI's control. This does not constitute a delay in delivery.

4 7-Day Satisfaction Refund Policy

If you are dissatisfied with work delivered, we offer a 7-day refund review period under the following conditions:

A refund request must:

  • Be submitted within 7 calendar days of service delivery
  • Clearly explain why the delivered work is unsatisfactory
  • Allow our team reasonable opportunity to correct or revise the issue before refund approval

Refunds may be approved if the delivered work materially differs from what was agreed, quality falls substantially below agreed standards, or AutoSageAI fails to deliver without valid reason.

Send requests to tonybuthel@gmail.com or WhatsApp 066 001 8931.

5 Non-Refundable Situations

Refunds will NOT be granted if:

  • Work has been fully approved by the customer
  • The customer changes project requirements after work has commenced
  • Delays are caused by the customer's failure to provide required materials
  • Subscription billing periods have already been consumed
  • Digital products, source code, or completed automation systems have been delivered and accepted
  • The customer simply changes their mind after work has begun
  • DNS propagation or third-party platform delays occur after correct setup

6 Cancellation Policy

  • Monthly subscriptions require cancellation before the next billing cycle
  • Annual subscriptions must be cancelled before the renewal date
  • Cancellation stops future billing only — previous payments are subject to the refund policy
  • Cancellation requests must be submitted via email or WhatsApp

7 Customer Responsibilities

  • Provide accurate business information and content when requested
  • Respond promptly during project development (within 48 hours)
  • Supply required content, branding, documents, credentials, or approvals when needed
  • Use all AutoSageAI services legally, ethically, and in compliance with South African law
  • Not use AutoSageAI platforms or systems for unlawful, harmful, or deceptive purposes

8 Intellectual Property

  • All custom work (websites, content, design) becomes the customer's property after final payment
  • Proprietary systems, frameworks, and automation engines created by AutoSageAI remain our intellectual property
  • Customers may not resell, reverse-engineer, or redistribute AutoSageAI's proprietary systems without written consent

9 Limitation of Liability

AutoSageAI is not responsible for third-party software outages, hosting provider failures, DNS propagation delays, or losses from misuse. Our total liability is limited to the amount paid for the specific service in question.

10 Payment Processing

All payments are processed through approved third-party providers. AutoSageAI does not store customer banking details. Payments accepted include EFT, bank transfer, and PayFast.

11 Fraud Prevention

AutoSageAI reserves the right to refuse suspicious transactions, verify customer identity, cancel fraudulent purchases without refund, and report suspected fraud to South African authorities.

12 Changes to These Terms

We may update these Terms at any time. Updated versions are published on our website and become effective immediately. Continued use of our services constitutes acceptance of revised terms.

13 Contact & Disputes

  • Email: tonybuthel@gmail.com
  • WhatsApp: 066 001 8931
  • Website: www.autosageai.co.za
  • Location: Johannesburg, South Africa
